title: Oreocharis pumila
intro: Oreocharis pumila is a small, perennial plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Gesneriaceae. The health benefits of Oreocharis pumila include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Oreocharis pumila is commonly used in traditional medicine to make herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
